name: BibLib

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# BibLib 1.8.2

## Fixed

- Restored mobile availability by marking BibLib as not desktop-only.
- Desktop-only Zotero connector functionality remains gated on mobile, so mobile users can still use the plugin features that are supported there.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# BibLib 1.8.1

## Changed

- Added the official Obsidian plugin ESLint checks used for community plugin review and fixed BibLib so the full rule set passes without disabled or weakened rules.
- Raised the minimum Obsidian version to 1.6.6 to match the plugin APIs used by BibLib.
- Marked the plugin as desktop-only to reflect its current connector and local-file functionality.
- Updated the build configuration to use Node's built-in module list directly instead of the `builtin-modules` package.

## Fixed

- (#25) Auto-fill section now displays correctly on Android mobile.
  - Replaced the native `<details>` element with a custom collapsible for cross-platform compatibility.
  - Android WebView was not rendering the native `<details>/<summary>` elements correctly.
  - Thanks to @jmroberts439 for reporting.
- (#29) BibTeX export now preserves citation keys from literature note frontmatter.
  - Previously, citation-js would regenerate keys such as `Smith2023Study` instead of preserving keys like `smith2023`.
  - The `id` field is now copied to `citation-key` before export.
  - Special characters in keys such as hyphens and colons are now preserved.
  - Thanks to @mobius-eng for reporting.
- Whitespace-only Zotero keys now fall back to generated citekeys instead of producing an empty citekey.
- Improved type handling around Citation.js, Zotero data, CSL dates, modal form values, and template playground sample data.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Biblib 1.8.0

## Added

- (#12) **Multiple tags support**: The "Literature note tag" setting now supports multiple comma or space-separated tags (e.g., "literature_note, excalidraw"). All specified tags are added to new literature notes, enabling workflows like Excalidraw flip notes.
  - Thanks to @bepolymathe for the feature request
- **Testing infrastructure**: Jest testing framework with initial test suites for citekey generator and template engine
- **Expanded test coverage**: Added 127 new tests across 5 test files (190 total tests), covering yaml-utils, citoid service, and template-variable-builder-service
- **Integration tests for citation mapping**: Added 46 integration tests covering the complete Zotero-to-CSL mapping workflow, including all item types and edge cases
- **End-to-end testing**: Playwright-based e2e tests that run against a real Obsidian instance
  - Tests command palette integration, modal workflows, and DOI lookup
  - Automated screenshots captured at key points for visual verification
  - Linux support via CDP connection to unpacked AppImage
- **New template variable `authorsDisplay`**: Formatted author string for display (e.g., "J. Smith", "J. Smith and B. Jones", "J. Smith et al.")
- (#21) **Citation-js fallback for Citoid service**: When Citoid (Wikipedia's citation API) lacks coverage for certain items (e.g., some ISBNs no longer available via WorldCat), the plugin now falls back to citation-js using Google APIs
  - Thanks to @platon-ivanov for this contribution
- (#21) **Additional identifier support**: PubMed IDs, PMC IDs, and Wikidata QIDs are now supported via the citation-js fallback
  - Thanks to @platon-ivanov for this contribution
- Comprehensive JSDoc documentation throughout the codebase

## Changed

- **Simplified literature note modal UI**: Cleaner design using Obsidian's default styling
  - Collapsible auto-fill section uses native `<details>` element
  - Single date input accepts flexible formats (YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD)
  - Attachment UI simplified to two buttons: "Import file" and "Link file"
  - Form fields stack vertically for consistent input widths
- **Reduced CSS footprint**: styles.css reduced from ~1500 to ~750 lines by using Obsidian defaults
- Improved MetadataCache usage patterns
- Enhanced error handling and validation in citoid service

## Fixed

- (#18) **Filename template: author variables now work** - The `{{authors}}`, `{{author}}`, `{{authors_family}}`, and `{{authors_given}}` template variables now work in filename templates. Previously these variables were not populated when generating literature note filenames.
- **Template engine: trailing whitespace preserved in formatter arguments** - Formatters like `join`, `prefix`, and `split` now correctly preserve trailing whitespace (e.g., `{{authors|join: and }}` produces "Smith and Jones" instead of "SmithandJones")
- (#11) **Template engine: object property access in array iteration** - When iterating over arrays of objects, properties are now directly accessible (e.g., `{{#authors}}{{family}}{{/authors}}` works correctly)
  - Thanks to @bepolymathe for reporting
- (#16) **Documentation fixes**: Fixed broken links and aligned documentation with actual plugin UI
  - Fixed 404 errors for Templating, Key Features, and Troubleshooting pages
  - Updated Settings documentation to match the actual tab names and order in the plugin
  - Section names now match the UI: "File Organization", "Templates", "Citation Keys", "Custom Fields", "Modal Configuration", "Zotero Integration", "Bibliography Export"
  - Thanks to @chenghuang-coder for reporting

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
feat: Add ability to link related notes to bibliography entries

This commit introduces the capability for users to link existing notes
within their Obsidian vault to newly created bibliography or chapter
entries. This allows for better organization and connection between
reference items and related thoughts, summaries, or project notes.

**Key Changes:**

1.  **UI Enhancements (Bibliography & Chapter Modals):**
    *   Added a new "Related Notes" section to both the
`BibliographyModal` and `ChapterModal`.
    *   This section includes:
        *   An "Add Related Note" button that triggers a note selection
interface.
        *   A display area showing currently selected related notes
(displaying the basename for readability).
        *   A "Remove" button next to each selected note in the display
area.
    *   Introduced a new state variable `relatedNotePaths` in both
modals to store the paths of selected notes.
    *   Implemented `updateRelatedNotesDisplay` helper function to
manage the UI for displaying and removing selected notes.
    *   Minor UI rearrangement in `BibliographyModal`: moved the
Identifier/Citekey field below the main content fields and related notes
section for better flow.

2.  **Note Selection Modal (`NoteSuggestModal`):**
    *   Created a new reusable `NoteSuggestModal` (extending
`FuzzySuggestModal`).
    *   This modal specifically filters for and allows fuzzy searching
of `.md` files within the vault.
    *   It takes an `onSelect` callback to handle the chosen file.

3.  **Data Flow Integration:**
    *   Modified `CreateNoteInput` interface in
`note-creation-service.ts` to include an optional `relatedNotePaths`
array.
    *   Updated `NoteCreationService` to accept `relatedNotePaths` and
pass them down to the `NoteContentBuilderService`.
    *   Modified `NoteContentInput` and `FrontmatterInput` interfaces to
include `relatedNotePaths`.
    *   Updated `NoteContentBuilderService` and
`FrontmatterBuilderService` to receive `relatedNotePaths` and pass them
to the `TemplateVariableBuilderService`.

4.  **Template Variable Expansion:**
    *   Updated `TemplateVariableBuilderService` (`buildVariables`
function) to accept an optional `relatedNotePaths` array.
    *   Added new template variables:
        *   `{{links}}`: An array of Obsidian wikilinks (e.g., `["[[Note
A]]", "[[Folder/Note B]]"]`). Suitable for YAML lists.
        *   `{{linkPaths}}`: An array of raw file paths (e.g., `["Note
A.md", "Folder/Note B.md"]`).
        *   `{{links_string}}`: A comma-separated string of Obsidian
wikilinks (e.g., `"[[Note A]], [[Folder/Note B]]"`). Suitable for inline
use.
    *   Ensured these variables default to empty values if no related
notes are selected.

5.  **Default Settings Update:**
    *   Changed the default template for the `related` custom
frontmatter field in `settings.ts` from `'[]'` to `'[{{links}}]'`. This
automatically formats selected related notes as a YAML list of wikilinks
in the frontmatter by default.

6.  **Settings Documentation:**
    *   Updated the template variable guide in the
`BibliographySettingTab` to include descriptions of the new `{{links}}`,
`{{linkPaths}}`, and `{{links_string}}` variables.

**Motivation:**

This feature addresses the need to create explicit connections between
bibliography items managed by the plugin and other relevant notes within
the user's vault, enhancing the knowledge graph and research workflow.
Users can now easily link references to their literature reviews,
project outlines, or concept notes directly during creation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/callumalpass/obsidian-biblib/compare/1.0.3...1.1.0

This release adds commands which allow importing a large number of literature notes from bibtex and csl files. This should be helpful for users migrating from reference managers like Zotero. 

It is recommended that, if you are coming from Zotero, you export your library using Zotero's native bibtex export. When Zotero exports to bibtex, you are offered the option of exporting annotations (notes) as well as attachments. The bulk import command can now optionally import these notes (to the body of the new literature notes) as well as attachments (to your configured literature note attachment directory).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Version 1.0.1

This is a major release introducing significant new features, extensive refactoring for flexibility and consistency, and numerous improvements across the plugin. Given the scope of these changes, we are moving to version 1.0.1.

### Major Features & Changes

**1. Unified Templating System**

*   Introduced a powerful, unified templating engine inspired by Mustache/Handlebars syntax (`{{variable}}`).
*   This engine now powers:
    *   Literature Note Headers (`headerTemplate`)
    *   Custom Frontmatter Field values
    *   Citekey Generation (`citekeyTemplate`)
*   Supports advanced features consistently across all template areas:
    *   Variable substitution (e.g., `{{title}}`, `{{author.family}}`)
    *   Conditional blocks (`{{#variable}}...{{/variable}}`, `{{^variable}}...{{/variable}}`)
    *   Looping over arrays (`{{#authors}}...{{.}}...{{/authors}}`) with helpers (`@index`, `@first`, `@last`, etc.)
    *   Formatting pipes (e.g., `{{title|lowercase}}`, `{{authors_family|abbr3}}`, `{{issued.date-parts.0.0|date}}`)
*   Provides access to a wide range of data: CSL fields, structured contributor arrays (`authors_family`, `editors_given`, etc.), `pdflink` (raw path), `currentDate`, and more.
*   Removed previous distinct template logic from various parts of the plugin.
*   Added comprehensive documentation guides for the template system directly within the plugin settings tab.

**2. Custom Frontmatter Fields**

*   Replaced legacy toggles (`includeDateCreated`, `includeYear`, `includeAuthorLink`, etc.) with a flexible, template-based custom frontmatter field system.
*   Users can now define any number of custom fields in settings, each with a name, a template for its value, and an enabled/disabled toggle.
*   **YAML Array Support:** Templates starting with `[` and ending with `]` are parsed intelligently to create proper YAML arrays in frontmatter (e.g., for `aliases`, `tags`, lists of links).
*   Default settings now provide examples for `year`, `dateCreated`, `status`, `aliases`, `author-links`, `attachment`, `keywords`, and `related` using the new system.
*   Hardcoded logic for adding specific fields (year, dateCreated, etc.) has been removed; control is now entirely via templates.
*   Added a detailed "Custom Frontmatter Fields Guide" in the settings tab, including YAML array examples.

**3. Template-Based Citekey Generation**

*   Introduced a `citekeyTemplate` setting, allowing users to define citekey formats using the unified templating engine (e.g., `{{authors_family.0|lowercase}}{{issued.date-parts.0.0}}`).
*   Added citekey-specific formatters like `|abbr3`, `|abbr4`, `|titleword`, `|shorttitle`.
*   **Legacy Fallback:** Previous citekey generation options (author abbreviation style, delimiters, multiple author handling, etc.) are now hidden by default but remain functional *only* if the `citekeyTemplate` field is left empty, ensuring backward compatibility.
*   The settings UI dynamically shows/hides legacy options and provides guidance.
*   Added a "Citekey Generation Guide" to the settings tab.
*   Refined citekey generation logic to ensure an `id` is always present and respects the `useZoteroKeys` setting appropriately within the legacy fallback or template context (default for `useZoteroKeys` is now `false`).

**4. Zotero Connector Integration (Desktop Only)**

*   Implemented a local server within the plugin that can listen for requests from the Zotero Connector browser extension (mimicking the Zotero desktop client).
*   Allows capturing citations and available PDFs directly from the browser into Obsidian *without* needing the Zotero desktop application running.
*   Automatically downloads linked PDFs to a temporary location.
*   Opens the "Create Literature Note" modal pre-filled with captured citation data and the downloaded PDF as an attachment.
*   Added settings to enable/disable the server, configure the port, and set the temporary download folder.
*   Updated README and settings tab with detailed setup instructions.

**5. Enhanced Zotero Data Handling & Parsing**

*   Significantly refactored Zotero JSON import processing.
*   Implemented a comprehensive, rule-based mapping system (`ZOTERO_MAPPING`) to translate Zotero item types and fields much more accurately and completely to CSL-JSON.
*   Includes specific converters for complex fields like creators (handling roles, institutional authors), dates, and tags.
*   Added support for parsing key-value pairs from the Zotero "extra" field, mapping known keys (e.g., "Original Date", "PMID") to CSL fields and merging them into the final data.
*   Integrated `@citation-js/date` for improved standardized date parsing, with robust fallbacks for various formats (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, YYYY).
*   This custom Zotero parsing is now prioritized for Zotero JSON data, with Citation.js internal parsing used as a fallback.

**6. User Interface & Experience Improvements**

*   **Settings Tab:** Restructured for clarity, removing legacy options and adding expandable, detailed guides for the Template System, Custom Frontmatter, and Citekey Generation. Improved UI for managing custom frontmatter fields.
*   **Bibliography Modal:**
    *   The "Auto-fill" section (Citoid/BibTeX paste) is now collapsible, reducing clutter, especially when opened via the Zotero Connector. Its initial state depends on the opening context.
    *   Improved layout by moving the Attachment section higher.
    *   Added visual indicators for attachments originating from Zotero.
*   **Chapter Modal:** Replaced the basic text input for linking existing file attachments with a user-friendly fuzzy file suggester, allowing easy searching of vault files.
*   **Error Handling:** Improved error messages and console logs provide more context.

### Other Improvements

*   **`pdflink` Template Variable:** The `{{pdflink}}` variable now provides the *raw attachment path* (if available), giving users full control over how to format links (e.g., `[[{{pdflink}}|{{citekey}}]]`) within templates. Default templates have been updated accordingly.
*   **Default Type:** Ensures CSL entries always have a `type` field, defaulting to `document` if parsing fails to determine one.
*   **Code Structure:** Consolidated related logic (e.g., Zotero mapping, templating) and removed redundant code.
*   **Styling:** Numerous CSS improvements for settings UI, documentation guides, custom fields, modal elements, etc., enhancing readability and appearance.

### Potential Breaking Changes / Migration Notes

*   The old frontmatter toggles (Include Year, Include Author Link, etc.) have been removed. Please migrate your desired frontmatter content to the new **Custom Frontmatter Fields** setting using templates. Default custom fields mimicking the old behavior are provided.
*   Citekey generation now primarily uses the **`citekeyTemplate`** setting. If you relied on the previous detailed options, leave the template field *empty* to continue using the legacy system.
*   The default setting for **`useZoteroKeys`** is now `false`. If you prefer using Zotero's own keys as citekeys by default, you need to enable this option (and ensure the `citekeyTemplate` is empty).
*   The **`{{pdflink}}`** template variable now contains the raw file path, not a pre-formatted `[[wikilink]]`. Update your header templates if you were relying on the old behavior (e.g., change `# {{pdflink}}` to `# [[{{pdflink}}|{{citekey}}]]`).

This release marks a major step forward in flexibility, integration, and user control. We hope you find these new features powerful and useful for your workflow!

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/callumalpass/obsidian-biblib/compare/0.3.4...1.0.1
